Metadata.title: Coat of Arms — Handcrafted European Copper Plate Art
Metadata.description: Limited, hand-worked copper impressions of European city coats of arms—made to be collected, gifted and kept.

Announcement: HANDCRAFTED · LIMITED BY CITY · SECURE WORLDWIDE DELIVERY

Home.eyebrow: European city archives · made by hand
Home.title: Time Patinas Copper.
Home.intro: Each plate begins with a civic emblem and ends as a singular surface—hammered, oxidised, signed and numbered by hand.
Home.proofEditionText: Only ten impressions are made for each city, each bearing its own handwritten number.
Home.proofHandText: Pressure, heat and patina leave marks that no machine can repeat.
Home.proofArchiveText: Heraldry becomes a tangible record of the city that formed it.
Home.featuredTitle: The first chapter of the archive
Home.featuredIntro: Nine places, nine distinct emblems. Each work carries the tone of its city and the hand that shaped it.
Home.archiveTitle: Follow the old routes through Bohemia
Home.archiveText: From Prague’s towers to Kutná Hora’s silver story, the collection reads like an atlas made in copper.
Home.craftIntro: The process is measured, but its traces stay alive: a crease, an oxidised edge, the quiet evidence of pressure.
Home.storyTitle: A small monument for an everyday room
Home.storyText: City arms were once carved above gates, printed on documents and carried on seals. We return them to the scale of the hand—objects that can move between homes while keeping their sense of place.
Home.ctaTitle: Choose the city you wish to keep.
Home.ctaText: Every piece is packed with its numbered certificate and a note on the emblem’s history.

CraftSteps.oneText: A raw sheet is cut and prepared. Its existing grain becomes part of the finished work.
CraftSteps.twoText: The city emblem is worked into the metal by hand, building relief through pressure and repeated touch.
CraftSteps.threeText: Heat, oxidation and pigments deepen the surface. Variations are welcomed, never hidden.
CraftSteps.fourText: The final plate is inspected, signed and marked 1/10 before it enters the archive.

Collection.title: Nine cities, held in copper
Collection.intro: Filter by place, then enter each object record for its story, edition and making notes.

Cities.title: A city archive in nine entries
Cities.intro: This is not a map of distance but of memory: a route between gates, lions, roses, mines and rivers.

Craft.title: Every mark is part of the record
Craft.intro: These works are not mechanically identical decorations. Their value lies in the meeting of a historic emblem with an unrepeatable hand-worked surface.
Craft.traceText: Oxidation, fine scratches, hammer marks, small rises and colour shifts belong to the life of the copper. No two numbered works will age in exactly the same way.
Craft.certificateText: Each object is paired with a numbered certificate recording its city, edition and work details.

About.title: Keeping the memory of a city within reach
About.p1: Coat of Arms began with a simple observation: a city’s emblem can hold centuries of local identity in one compact image. Yet these signs are often passed without being seen.
About.p2: We work them into copper so their lines can be encountered again—as relief, reflection and patina. The slow process makes each piece both an archive and an original surface.
About.p3: The edition of ten is intentionally small. Numbering records scarcity, but also relationship: each work belongs to a family while carrying marks that are entirely its own.
About.studioText: Artist and studio details will be documented here once confirmed. We do not invent biographies; the object and its process remain the present record.

Contact.title: Collecting, collaboration and city requests
Contact.intro: For questions about the archive, commissions or partnerships, send a note below. For an existing purchase, please use the checkout flow so we receive the complete delivery record.
Contact.reply: We usually reply within two working days.
Contact.name: Name
Contact.subject: Subject
Contact.success: Your note has been received. We will reply shortly.
Contact.messagePlaceholder: Tell us what you would like to ask.

Product.authText: Each piece is signed and numbered by hand and travels with a matching certificate of authenticity.
Product.traceText: Oxidation, fine scratches, raised edges and colour variation are intentional records of the making process—not defects.
Product.deliveryText: Packed in archival tissue with reinforced corner protection. Delivery timing and import requirements are confirmed before dispatch. Returns are accepted according to the condition terms in the final order.

Cart.title: Cart
Cart.emptyText: Choose a city plate to begin a collection.
Cart.note: No payment session can be created until your contact and delivery details are validated.

Checkout.contactIntro: Because each object is limited and requires careful packing, we confirm a complete contact and delivery record before payment.

Checkout.reviewTitle: Please read this record before payment
